Current Operations

    Other Operations

  • Coast Guard offloads over $19.3 million in illicit drugs interdicted in Caribbean Sea US Coast Guard 23 Apr 2026 -- U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Resolute's crew offloaded approximately 2,570 pounds of cocaine worth more than $19.3 million and transferred six suspected drug smugglers to federal authorities, Thursday, at Base Miami Beach.

    • Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Is The US Naval Blockade On Iran Working? Yes And No. RFE/RL 23 Apr 2026 -- To pressure Iran into a peace deal, the United States has imposed a naval blockade on the Middle Eastern country's ports and vessels since April 13.

  • Trump Says Ordered US Navy to Triple Mine Clearing Activity in Strait of Hormuz Sputnik 23 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Thursday he is ordering the United States Navy to triple mine clearing activity in the Strait of Hormuz.
  • Pentagon Disputes Report Saying Hormuz Mine Clearance Could Take Six Months RFE/RL 23 Apr 2026 -- The Pentagon has disputed an article by The Washington Post which said that clearing Iranian mines from the Strait of Hormuz could take six months.
  • US intercepts three Iranian oil tankers in Asian waters, sources say Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 23 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. military has intercepted at least three Iranian-flagged tankers in ‌Asian waters and is redirecting them away from their positions near India, Malaysia and Sri Lanka, according to Reuters.

  • Report: Container Ships Seized By Tehran Taken To Iranian Port RFE/RL 23 Apr 2026 -- Two container ships seized by Iran near the Strait of Hormuz with about 40 crew aboard have been taken toward the Iranian port of Bandar Abbas, sources told Reuterson April 23 after Tehran vowed retaliation for the US seizure of an Iranian vessel three days earlier.
  • Panama Calls Seizure Of Ship In Strait Of Hormuz 'Illegal' RFE/RL 23 Apr 2026 -- Panama condemned the seizure of a ship flying its flag as it passed through the Strait of Hormuz, calling it an "illegal detention."

  • Trump Rules Out Use Of Nuclear Weapons Against Iran RFE/RL 23 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said he has ruled out the use of nuclear weapons against Iran and also told reporters, "Don't rush me," when asked how long he was willing to wait for a peace deal as the war passed its 54th day.
  • Trump Says Deal With Iran Will Be Made When 'Appropriate And Good' RFE/RL 23 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said a deal with Tehran will be made when it is "appropriate and good" for the United States, amid reports that the White House is seeking to fast-track an end to the US-Israeli war with Iran.
  • US Senate Rejects New Attempt To Limit Trump's Military Authority Against Iran RFE/RL 23 Apr 2026 -- The US Senate on April 22 voted 51-46 to reject a Democratic-led effort to limit President Donald Trump's authority to use military force against Iran.
  • U.S. State Department urges Americans to leave Iran after airspace reopening People's Daily 23 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. State Department on Wednesday urged U.S. citizens in Iran to leave after the Middle East country partially reopened its airspace.
  • Trump not viewing Iran's ships seizure near Hormuz Strait as breach of ceasefire: White House People's Daily 23 Apr 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump did not view Iran's seizure of two European ships by force near the Strait of Hormuz as a breach of the extended U.S. ceasefire with Iran, White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said on Wednesday.

  • Iranian Lawmaker Says Khamenei Opposes Continuing US Negotiations RFE/RL 23 Apr 2026 -- A member of the National Security Committee of the Iranian parliament said that Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei opposes extending negotiations with the United States.
  • More Than 10 Million Barrels Of Iranian Crude Left Persian Gulf Since Blockade Began RFE/RL 23 Apr 2026 -- An oil market data analysis firm reported on April 23 that "more than 10 million barrels of Iranian crude left the Persian Gulf" since the US military began its naval blockade of Iranian ports.
  • April 23: Iran's united leadership rebuffs US provocations as truce violations persist Press TV 23 Apr 2026 -- Iran's top leadership delivered a unified and powerful response to Washington on Thursday, dismissing US President Donald Trump's latest war rhetoric as a desperate attempt to sow division after facing the military and strategic defeat.
  • IRGC commander: Unyielding unity of people and officials defying US-Zionist enemy Press TV 23 Apr 2026 -- Brigadier General Esmail Qaani, Commander of the Quds Force of Iran's Islamic Revolution Guard Corps (IRGC), has stated that the unyielding unity displayed by the Iranian nation in the streets and the complete cohesion among state officials represent the decisive support for the Resistance Front and its borderless holy fighters in their sacred war against the American-Zionist enemy.
  • Leader: Remarkable unity among Iranians shatters enemy ranks Press TV 23 Apr 2026 -- Leader of the Islamic Revolution Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei has lauded the profound impact of the Iranian nation's extraordinary unity, saying that it has inflicted a significant fracture on the enemy while urging practical gratitude to forge even stronger national cohesion.
  • Iran central bank confirms Strait of Hormuz fees received in cash Press TV 23 Apr 2026 -- Iran's central bank has confirmed the successful deposit of revenue from newly established transit fees for ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz, ending media speculation about the currency involved, the Fars news agency reports.
  • IRGC speedboats, UUVs waiting for intruding US vessels: Judiciary chief Press TV 23 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Judiciary Chief Gholam-Hossein Mohseni-Eje'i says the speedboats and Unmanned Underwater Vehicles (UUVs) of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) are waiting for intruding US vessels to fully destroy them.
  • End Of Iran Sanctions Waiver Sharpens Focus On Financial Pressure RFE/RL 23 Apr 2026 -- The expiration of a US sanctions waiver on Iranian oil last weekend has brought renewed attention to Washington's economic strategy, as policymakers seek to limit Tehran's access to revenue while navigating global energy market pressures.
  • Tehran protests to UN over US misuse of Arab states' soil for anti-Iran attacks Press TV 23 Apr 2026 -- The Iranian ambassador and permanent representative to the United Nations has strongly protested to the world body over the United States' misuse of the airspace and territory of five Persian Gulf Arab countries for illegal strikes on the Islamic Republic during the imposed war.

  • Middle East war: After oil and gas, concerns grow over minerals crunch UN News 23 Apr 2026 -- The shipping crisis in the Strait of Hormuz caused by war in the Middle East has exposed a new threat: a looming shortage of strategic minerals that drive economies all over the world - and a race by countries to obtain them.
  • Despite ceasefire, Hormuz tensions continue to throttle supply chains worldwide UN News 23 Apr 2026 -- A temporary ceasefire between the United States and Iran has been extended, offering an opening for diplomacy - but persistent tensions in the Strait of Hormuz are compounding trade disruptions and intensifying pressure on humanitarian operations and vulnerable communities far beyond the Gulf.

  • IEA's Birol warns of 'biggest energy security threat in history' Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 23 Apr 2026 -- International Energy Agency (IEA) Executive Director Fatih Birol reiterated his warnings related to ongoing global disruptions in energy flows amid conflict in the Middle East, suggesting that the world is facing "the biggest energy security threat in history", the Daily Sabah reported.

  • China's four-point proposal: a constructive contribution to Middle East peace and stability People's Daily 23 Apr 2026 -- During a recent meeting with Sheikh Khaled bin Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, Crown Prince of Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates (UAE), Chinese President Xi Jinping put forward a four-point proposal on promoting peace and stability in the Middle East.

  • Meeting between Special Envoy of Minister of Foreign Affairs and Iranian Minister of Foreign Affairs (April 22) ROK MFA 23 Apr 2026
  • Europe cracks: French president rejects coercive US blockades against Iran Press TV 23 Apr 2026 -- French President Emmanuel Macron has publicly rejected Washington's coercive blockades against the Islamic Republic of Iran, declaring that negotiations must be "systematic" and rooted in diplomacy, not military pressure or economic strangulation.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • U.S. Soldier Charged With Using Classified Information To Profit From Prediction Market Bets US Dept. of Justice 23 Apr 2026 -- Gannon Ken Van Dyke allegedly made more than $400,000 trading on polymarket on the basis of classified information regarding the timing of a U.S. military operation to capture Nicolás Maduro in Venezuela

  • Department of War Showcases Progress on Golden Dome for America, the Nation's Next-Generation Homeland Defense DoD 23 Apr 2026 -- Senior leaders from the Department of War, U.S. government, and defense industry gathered at Joint Expeditionary Base Little Creek-Fort Story, April 23, to provide an update about Golden Dome for America (GDA), the nation's initiative to build a next-generation missile defense shield to protect the Homeland.
  • USAF, Netherlands formalize partnership on Collaborative Combat Aircraft development US Air Force 23 Apr 2026 -- The Department of the Air Force and the Netherlands Ministry of Defence have formalized a partnership to acquire prototype Collaborative Combat Aircraft, advancing a shared strategy to bring greater affordable mass to deter, and if necessary, defeat adversaries.
  • Exercise Obangame Express Returns to Cameroon for 15th Iteration US Navy 23 Apr 2026 -- Obangame Express 2026 (OE 26), sponsored by U.S. Africa Command and conducted by the U.S. Navy's Sixth Fleet, held an opening ceremony for the exercise April 23, 2026 in host nation Cameroon.
  • USS Blue Ridge conducts consecutive combined maritime operations with allies in Western Pacific Ocean US Navy 23 Apr 2026 -- U.S. 7th Fleet flagship Blue Ridge-class amphibious command ship USS Blue Ridge (LCC 19) conducted a series of bilateral and multilateral maritime exercises with allies across the Western Pacific Ocean, April 13-17.
  • Senior enlisted leaders emphasize partnership as cornerstone of space superiority United States Space Force 23 Apr 2026 -- In a panel discussion at the 41st Space Symposium on April 15, senior enlisted leaders from the United States and Canada asserted that robust partnerships with industry, allies, and technology partners are fundamental to maintaining a competitive advantage in the space domain.
  • US Secretary of Navy Resigns - Department of War Sputnik 23 Apr 2026 -- US Secretary of the Navy John Phelan has resigned, the Department of War said.
  • US Navy Secretary John C. Phelan departing administration: Pentagon Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 23 Apr 2026 -- US Secretary of the Navy John C. Phelan is departing the administration "effective immediately," Anadolu Agency reported citing Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnel.
  • Hegseth fires Navy Secretary Phelan in another sudden Department of War shake-up Press TV 23 Apr 2026 -- Pete Hegseth has removed US Navy Secretary John Phelan, the latest casualty of the US secretary of war's abrupt dismissal campaign amid reported top-level opposition to Washington's unprovoked aggression against Iran.
  • US suspends arms deliveries to NATO ally Estonia as war on Iran draining US arsenal Press TV 23 Apr 2026 -- The United States has suspended arms deliveries to NATO ally Estonia as its aggression on Iran has put a major strain on US weapons stockpiles.
  • Nevada Guard maintenance unit deploys to support CENTCOM Army News 23 Apr 2026 -- The 150th Maintenance Company has deployed to the Middle East to support operations in the U.S. Central Command area of responsibility, marking the unit's first deployment.
  • HIMARS unit fires simultaneously across 700 miles, three states Army News 23 Apr 2026 -- On April 17, Lt. Col. Edward Guelfi gave the command for his battalion to fire at a simulated target at the Idaho Army National Guard's Camp Orchard. Standing just south of Boise, he watched eight M142 High Mobility Artillery Rocket Systems fire two rounds each about 800 meters in front of him.

  • Russia Raises Alarm Over NATO Drills Aiming to Strangle Kaliningrad Region Sputnik 23 Apr 2026 -- Western countries are rehearsing scenarios for a naval blockade and the seizure of the Kaliningrad region during the Joint Expeditionary Force exercises, Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Alexander Grushko told Sputnik.
  • Russian Aerospace Forces successfully launch Angara-1.2 carrier rocket from Plesetsk cosmodrome Russia MoD 23 Apr 2026 -- On Thursday, 23 April, at 11:29 (Moscow time), a combat crew of the Russian Aerospace Forces successfully launched an Angara-1.2 light-class carrier rocket with spacecraft in the interest of the Russian Defence Ministry from the State Test Cosmodrome (Plesetsk Cosmodrome) of the Russian Defence Ministry in Arkhangelsk region.

  • 'Busification' Is A Dirty Word In Ukraine -- And A Symptom Of Its Persistent Problem With Military Manpower RFE/RL 23 Apr 2026 -- The gunshots rang out amid stopped midday traffic, panicking bystanders on a busy road in the Ukrainian Black Sea city of Odesa. Masked men wearing bulletproof vests pulled other men from a white minivan, throwing them to the pavement and pounding on them to make them stay still.
  • Defense Support and Development of Joint Weapons Production: Presidents of Ukraine and Lithuania Held a Meeting President of Ukraine 23 Apr 2026
  • Yevhen Perebyinis held a meeting with the Chairman of the EU Military Committee, General Sean Clancy Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine 23 Apr 2026
  • Over 181,000 drones, UGVs, EW systems, and other equipment were supplied to the front via the e-Points system in 2026 Ukraine MOD 23 Apr 2026 -- Since the beginning of 2026, units of the Defence Forces of Ukraine have ordered drones, unmanned ground vehicles (UGVs), electronic warfare (EW) systems, and other equipment through the Brave1 Market using e-Points, totaling UAH 14 billion. More than 181,000 pieces of equipment have already been delivered to the battlefield.
  • EU and Ukraine launch programme worth over €160 million to advance dual-use technologies Ukraine MOD 23 Apr 2026 -- The European Commission and the Ministry of Defence of Ukraine have launched a cooperation initiative to invest in innovative and cutting-edge technologies. The agreement was concluded during the EU-Ukraine business summit in Brussels.
  • "Bizon" enters the battlefield: Defence Forces receive a UGV capable of carrying up to 300 kg Ukraine MOD 23 Apr 2026 -- The Ministry of Defence has codified and authorized the "Bizon-L" unmanned ground vehicle system for operational use within the Armed Forces of Ukraine.
  • NATO and the European Union call for more sustained, coordinated support to Ukraine at joint meeting NATO 23 Apr 2026 -- On Thursday (23 April 2026), at the Egmont Palace in Brussels, NATO Deputy Secretary General Radmila Shekerinska, together with the Chair of the Political and Security Committee of the European Union, Lene Mandel Vensild, led an informal meeting of the North Atlantic Council and the Political and Security Committee, with a focus on Ukraine.
  • Council finalises €90 billion support loan to Ukraine EC/CEU 23 Apr 2026 -- The Council today adopted the final key piece of legislation underpinning a €90 billion EU loan to Ukraine, agreed by the European Council in December 2025. The move will allow the Commission to begin disbursements as soon as possible in the second quarter of 2026.
  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna: breakthrough on €90 billion loan proves EU remains on course in supporting Ukraine Estonia MFA 23 Apr 2026 -- According to Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna, the agreement reached today, 23 April, in the European Union on allocating a €90 billion loan to Ukraine and the political approval of the 20th sanctions package mark a long-awaited breakthrough, demonstrating that despite obstacles the Union remains firmly on course in its support for Ukraine.

  • GT on the spot: Qingzhou mine countermeasures ship opens to public for first time at anniversary event, showcasing PLA Navy's low-profile but vital force Global Times 23 Apr 2026 -- Marking the 77th anniversary of the People's Liberation Army (PLA) Navy on Thursday, the PLA Navy held open-ship events in more than a dozen cities nationwide, with over 40 active-duty vessels opened to the public. Several of them were opened to the public for the first time. Among them, the Type 081 mine countermeasures ship, the Qingzhou, which made its debut in Dalian, Northeast China's Liaoning Province, drew particular attention. Unlike aircraft carriers or large destroyers, mine countermeasures ships rarely appear in the public eye. Curious to learn more, the Global Times reporter stepped aboard the Qingzhou to explore the spirit behind this warship.
  • China marks navy's 77th anniversary amid growing role in safeguarding peace People's Daily 23 Apr 2026 -- China on Thursday marked the 77th anniversary of its navy, whose presence and growing strength have become an important force in maintaining regional and global stability.

  • U.S. official urges Taiwan parties to pass special defense budget CNA 23 Apr 2026 -- A senior official at the U.S. Department of Defense on Wednesday urged Taiwan's political parties to work together to approve a special defense budget, saying that the United States has multiple tools to provide security assistance to Taiwan.

  • Co-develop and co-produce with India in niche tech for secured national interests and global stability & resilience: Raksha Mantri to German industry captains in Munich India PIB 23 Apr 2026 -- Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh has invited the German industry to co-develop and co-produce with India, particularly in the field of niche technologies, as he underscored the need to forge partnerships based on reliability & shared interests, terming them as indispensable in the current shifts in geopolitical alignments, disruptions in supply chains, rapid technological transformations, and increasing global uncertainties. Shri Rajnath Singh was addressing Indian and German Defence Industry leaders during the Defence Investor Summit in Munich on April 23, 2026, the final day of his maiden visit to the European nation.
  • EMERGENCY LANDING FACILITY (ELF) ACTIVATION AT SULTANPUR ON PURVANCHAL EXPRESSWAY India PIB 23 Apr 2026 -- The Indian Air Force activated 'Emergency Landing Facility' (ELF) on the Purvanchal Expressway in Uttar Pradesh's Sultanpur District on 22 Apr 26, both by day and night, showcasing its operational capability to bolster its defence readiness. UP State Minister for Panchayati Raj and Minority Welfare Shri Om Prakash Rajbhar, Air Mshl B Manikantan, AOC-in-C, CAC, and other officials were present to witness the IAF aircraft undertake ELF operations.
  • 11th Joint Defence Committee meeting between India & Egypt held in Cairo India PIB 23 Apr 2026 -- The India-Egypt Joint Defence Committee (JDC) held productive discussions towards enhancing bilateral defence cooperation during its 11th meeting in Cairo from April 20 to 22, 2026. The Indian delegation was led by Joint Secretary (International Cooperation) Shri Amitabh Prasad and included senior representatives from the Ministry of Defence and the Defence Forces. The Egyptian delegation was headed by senior officials of the Defence Forces and the Ministry of Defence.

  • Israel MOD Procures Over $200M Elbit Systems Aerial Munitions Israel MOD 23 Apr 2026 -- As part of bolstering readiness for near-term combat scenarios and preparing for an intense security decade, the Israel Ministry of Defense (IMOD) Defense Procurement Directorate (DPD) has issued multi-year orders for aerial munitions manufactured by Elbit Systems, valued at over $200 million (approximately NIS 600 million).
  • Israel MOD Delivers IAI's BARAK MX to Slovak Armed Forces Israel MOD 23 Apr 2026 -- Under the 560 million euro agreement signed in 2024, the Israel Ministry of Defense (IMOD), through its Defense Export Directorate (SIBAT), together with Israel Aerospace Industries (IAI), announced today the delivery of the BARAK MX air-defense system to the Slovak Air Force.

  • Government decides to apply for additional funding for detecting and countering drones Finnish Government 23 Apr 2026 -- On 23 April, the Government decided that Finland would apply for EUR 35 million in additional funding from the European Commission to procure drone detection and counter-drone systems for the Finnish Border Guard.
  • Unauthorised possession of jammers to become a criminal offence Finnish Government 23 Apr 2026 -- On 23 April 2026, the Government submitted amendments to the Act on Electronic Communication Services for approval.
  • Government submits proposal to Parliament on amending Nuclear Energy Act and Criminal Code Finnish MoD 23 Apr 2026 -- On 23 April 2024, the Finnish Government submitted to Parliament a proposal to amend the Nuclear Energy Act and the Criminal Code.

  • Minister R. Kaunas and Minister of Defence of the Czech Republic agreed on cooperation in radar personnel training Lithuania MND 23 Apr 2026 -- Today, Minister of National Defence Robertas Kaunas has met with Minister of Defence of the Czech Republic Jaromír Zůna who came to visit with the Czech military personnel serving in the Multinational Battlegroup Lithuania in Rukla.
  • United States greenlights Lithuania's acquisition of approx. EUR 180 million worth of Sidewinder missiles Lithuania MND 23 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has approved the sale of AIM-9X Block II Sidewinder missiles to Lithuania. The U.S.-proposed contract on the missiles compatible with the NASAMS air defence system may stand at EUR 214 million (EUR 183 million).

  • Defence Minister's meeting with the Ambassador of the Hellenic Republic to Romania Romania - Ministry of National Defence 23 Apr 2026 -- On Thursday, April 23rd, Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, had a meeting with the Ambassador of the Hellenic Republic to Romania, H.E. Evangelia Lili Grammatika, at the MoND headquarters.
  • Meeting between the Chief of Defence Staff and the Chief of the General Staff of the Polish Armed Forces Romania - Ministry of National Defence 23 Apr 2026 -- On Wednesday, April 22nd, Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, and the Chief of Defence Staff, General Gheorghiță Vlad, met, at the MoND headquarters, with the Chief of General Staff of the Polish Armed Forces, General Wiesław Kukuła, who paid an official visit to Romania.

  • The Minister of Defence of the Republic of Armenia met with the Prime Minister of Georgia Ministry of Defence of the Republic of Armenia 23 Apr 2026
  • Armenia-Cyprus bilateral and Armenia-Greece-Cyprus trilateral defence consultations were held Ministry of Defence of the Republic of Armenia 23 Apr 2026 -- On April 22, Armenia-Cyprus bilateral and Armenia-Greece-Cyprus trilateral defence consultations were held in Cyprus, in which the delegation of the Ministry of Defence of the Republic of Armenia headed by Levon Ayvazyan, Head of the Defence Policy and International Cooperation Department participated.

  • Arctic Seven Meet to Advance Arctic Security US Dept. of State 23 Apr 2026 -- Senior diplomatic representatives from the Arctic Seven (A7) nations met today in Washington, DC, to discuss shared economic and security interests in the Arctic
  • United States Welcomes Jordan's Signing of the Artemis Accords US Dept. of State 23 Apr 2026 -- The Department of State congratulates the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan on joining the Artemis Accords. Jordan is the 63rd country to sign the Accords to date, pledging its commitment to peaceful space activities.
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with German State Secretary von Geyr US Dept. of State 23 Apr 2026

  • 23 April 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 23 Apr 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • Lebanese Official Says Beirut To Seek One-Month Cease-Fire Extension RFE/RL 23 Apr 2026 -- Lebanon and Israel are set to hold a second round of talks in Washington on April 23, with a Lebanese official saying Beirut will seek a one-month extension of a fragile US-mediated cease-fire between Hezbollah and Israel, which is due to expire on April 26.
  • Lebanese Prime Minister Rejects Deal With Israel Unless All Troops Are Pulled - Reports Sputnik 23 Apr 2026 -- The Lebanese government will not sign any agreement with Israel as part of negotiations unless it includes a "full withdrawal" of the Israeli troops from its territory, the Washington Post reported on Thursday, citing Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam.

  • Devastation at every turn greets UN aid mission to south Lebanon UN News 23 Apr 2026 -- A UNICEF relief mission reached a large hospital in the southern Lebanese town of Tebnine on Thursday, only to find massive destruction and staggering needs.

  • Joint Statement by the Foreign Ministers of Türkiye, Egypt, Indonesia, Jordan, Pakistan, Qatar, Saudi Arabia and UAE, 23 April 2026 Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 23 Apr 2026

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Syria, Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Central African Republic UNOCHA 23 Apr 2026

  • Chinese envoy to UN urges lasting ceasefire in Middle East, calls on Israel to withdraw from Syrian territory Global Times 23 Apr 2026 -- Tensions in the Middle East continue, with spillover effects adding new complexities to Syria's already fragile situation. China calls on the relevant parties to seize the window for peace, exercise maximum restraint, show utmost sincerity, and continue resolving disputes through political and diplomatic means in order to achieve a comprehensive and lasting ceasefire, Fu Cong, China's Permanent Representative to the United Nations, said at a UN meeting on Wednesday. He also noted that Israel should withdraw from Syrian territory without further delay.

  • ICC Pre-Trial Chamber I confirms all charges against Rodrigo Roa Duterte and commits him to trial ICC 23 Apr 2026 -- Today, 23 April 2026, Pre-Trial Chamber I of the International Criminal Court ("ICC" or "the Court") unanimously confirmed all the charges comprised of three counts of crimes against humanity brought by the Prosecutor against Rodrigo Roa Duterte and committed him to trial before a Trial Chamber.
  • ICC pre-trial chamber unanimously confirms all raps vs. Duterte Philippine News Agency 23 Apr 2026 -- The International Criminal Court (ICC) Pre-Trial Chamber I on Thursday has confirmed all charges against former president Rodrigo Duterte, and committed him to a trial.

  • Joint Statement on the Fifth Joint Oversight Committee for the Peace Agreement Between the Democratic Republic of the Congo and the Republic of Rwanda US Dept. of State 23 Apr 2026 -- The text of the following statement was released by the Governments of the United States of America, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, the Republic of Rwanda, the State of Qatar, the Republic of Togo (as the African Union mediator), and the African Union Commission on the occasion of the fifth Joint Oversight Committee held this week in Washington, DC.

  • Elections 'Only Credible Path' Back to Constitutional Order in Haiti, Mission Chief Tells Security Council, as Gang Suppression Force Speeds Deployment UN Security Council 23 Apr 2026 -- Despite positive political developments in Haiti, international security support is needed to create conditions conducive to holding planned elections, the Security Council heard today, as members debated how that support should operate in a context where minors are caught up in the gangs destabilizing the country.
  • Stability and lasting peace can only be achieved if the efforts of the international community are matched with action by all Haitian stakeholders: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 23 Apr 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador Archie Young, UK Deputy Permanent Representative to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Haiti.

News Reports

  • Chinese National Arrested at JFK International Airport on Federal Charges for Illegally Photographing Military Aircraft at Nebraska Air Force Base US Dept. of Justice 23 Apr 2026 -- Tianrui Liang, 21, of China, has been charged by complaint for conduct related to illegally documenting Air Force planes located at Offutt Air Force Base in Bellevue, Nebraska, announced United States Attorney Lesley A. Woods and Assistant Attorney General for National Security John A. Eisenberg.

  • Two Israeli air force soldiers face charges of spying for Iran: Report Press TV 23 Apr 2026 -- Israeli military prosecutors are preparing to file espionage charges against two Israeli air force soldiers accused of collaborating with Iranian intelligence, according to Israeli media reports.

  • Countering a Transnational Criminal Network Fueling America's Illicit Fentanyl Crisis US Dept. of State 23 Apr 2026 -- The United States is deploying targeted economic sanctions to disrupt a sophisticated transnational criminal network fueling America's illicit fentanyl crisis
  • Treasury Sanctions Global Illicit Drug Supply Chain Supporting the Sinaloa Cartel US Treasury 23 Apr 2026 -- Today, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctioned 23 individuals and entities that comprise a sophisticated synthetic opioid procurement network with ties to the Sinaloa Cartel, a designated Foreign Terrorist Organization. Chemical suppliers and brokers, including those designated today, have reshaped the narcotics landscape by enabling Mexican cartels to synthesize illicit drugs from precursor chemicals primarily imported from Asia. By

  • Meeting with Government members President of Russia 23 Apr 2026 -- Vladimir Putin chaired a videoconference meeting with Government members.
  • Arctic of Strategic Importance for Russia's Economic Development - Putin Sputnik 23 Apr 2026 -- The Arctic is of strategic importance for Russia's economic development and the protection of its sovereignty, Russian President Vladimir Putin said on Thursday.
  • Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine: 20th round of stern EU sanctions hits energy revenues, military industrial complex, trade and financial services including crypto EC/CEU 23 Apr 2026 -- The Council today adopted a far-reaching 20th package of restrictive measures comprising 120 further individual listings - the biggest package of listings in two years - and stern, multi-layered economic sanctions, targeting key sectors which fuel Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine.
  • EU adopts 20th package of sanctions against Russia European Commission 23 Apr 2026 -- The Commission welcomes the adoption by EU Member States of the 20th package of sanctions against Russia. The EU's commitment to a free and sovereign Ukraine is unwavering. This package puts further pressure on Russia to engage in negotiations and do so on terms acceptable for Ukraine
  • Questions and answers on the 20th package of sanctions against Russia European Commission 23 Apr 2026
  • European Union adopts new sanctions package against Russia Estonia MFA 23 Apr 2026 -- Today, 23 April, the European Union approved its 20th sanctions package aimed at reducing Russia's revenues and curbing its ability to continue its war of aggression against Ukraine.
  • Slovak minister says Russian oil supplies resumed via Druzhba pipeline Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 23 Apr 2026 -- The supply of Russian oil to Slovakia via the Druzhba pipeline has been resumed, the Slovak finance minister announced on Thursday, according to Anadolu Agency.
  • Meeting with President of Kyrgyzstan Sadyr Japarov President of Russia 23 Apr 2026 -- Vladimir Putin had a meeting with President of the Kyrgyz Republic Sadyr Japarov in the Kremlin.
  • A Drip, Drip Of Dissent In Russia: 'Minor Symptoms' Or Something More Serious? RFE/RL 23 Apr 2026 -- When the head of a Russian tractor manufacturer set off on the 370-kilometer journey to the Moscow Economic Forum earlier this month, he was about to put his company on the map with some stinging criticism of his country's authorities.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on April 23, 2026 PRC MofA 23 Apr 2026
  • China penalizes 56 provincial, ministerial-level officials in Q1 anti-graft drive People's Daily 23 Apr 2026 -- A total of 56 Chinese officials at the provincial and ministerial level received penalties in the first quarter (Q1) of 2026 in the country's anti-corruption fight, China's top anti-graft body said on Thursday.
  • Wang Yi Holds Talks with Cambodian Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation Prak Sokhonn PRC MofA 23 Apr 2026 -- On April 23, 2026, local time, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi held talks with Cambodian Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation Prak Sokhonn in Phnom Penh./li>
  • China, Cambodia hold 1st meeting between foreign, defense ministers under "2+2" strategic dialogue mechanism PRC MND 23 Apr 2026 -- The first meeting between foreign ministers and defense ministers of China and Cambodia under the "2+2" strategic dialogue mechanism was held here in Phnom Penh, capital of Cambodia, on Wednesday, aiming at enhancing bilateral cooperation in various fields.
  • Chinese state security authorities warn of industrial chain security risks after exposing insider espionage cases involving critical mineral, semiconductor and data resources Global Times 23 Apr 2026 -- China's Ministry of State Security (MSS) on Thursday disclosed several cases of leaks involving industrial and supply chains, which included insider leaks in semiconductor technology, data theft, and the covert extraction of critical mineral information.

  • U.S. blasts countries as serving China's interests by blocking Lai's flight CNA 23 Apr 2026 -- The United States government on Wednesday criticized the three African countries that revoked overflight clearances for President Lai Ching-te's (賴清德) planned trip to Eswatini this week, saying they had yielded to China's political demands.
  • Mainland slams DPP's political maneuvering over policy package to boost cross-Strait ties People's Daily 23 Apr 2026 -- A Chinese mainland spokesperson has slammed the political maneuvering by Taiwan's Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) authorities over the recent policy package unveiled by the mainland, which aims to boost ties with Taiwan.
  • Reunification only path for Taiwan, serves well-being of Taiwan compatriots: mainland spokesperson People's Daily 23 Apr 2026 -- Reunification is the only path forward for Taiwan and is key to the well-being of the people on the island, a mainland spokesperson said on Wednesday.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 23 Apr 2026

  • Jo Yong Won Meets Russian Delegation KCNA 23 Apr 2026 -- Jo Yong Won, member of the Presidium of the Political Bureau of the Central Committee of the Workers' Party of Korea, first vice-president of the State Affairs Commission of the DPRK and chairman of the Standing Committee of the Supreme People's Assembly, met with a delegation of the Ministry of Interior of the Russian Federation led by Minister Vladimir Kolokoltev which paid a courtesy call on him at the Pyongyang Assembly Hall on April 22.
  • Working-Level Talks between DPRK and Russia Held KCNA 23 Apr 2026 -- Working-level talks involving different sectors were held at Myongsasimni Hotel in the Wonsan Kalma coastal tourist area on April 22 to boost exchange and cooperation between the DPRK and the Russian Federation.
  • Both Sections of DPRK-Russia Border Motor Bridge Connected KCNA 23 Apr 2026 -- The project for connecting both sections of the DPRK-Russia border motor bridge was successfully carried out on April 21.

  • Spokesperson's Press Briefing (April 23, 2026) ROK MFA 23 Apr 2026

  • Iran Says It Has Executed Man For Ties To Banned Opposition Group RFE/RL 23 Apr 2026 -- Iran's judiciary announced on April 23 that it had executed a man after he was convicted of being a member of the People's Mojahedin Organization (MEK), a banned opposition group, and allegedly collaborating with Israeli security forces.
  • MKO-affiliated Mossad spy executed in Iran Press TV 23 Apr 2026 -- ‎A member of the terrorist Mujahedeen-e Khalq Organization (MKO), convicted of carrying out multiple attacks and spying for the Israeli regime's Mossad spy agency, has been executed after his death sentence was upheld by Iran's top tribunal.

  • Transcript of Weekly Media Briefing by the Official Spokesperson (April 23, 2026) India MEA 23 Apr 2026

  • Joint Statement on the Strategic Partnership Framework by the Foreign Ministers of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Republic of Türkiye, 23 April 2026 Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 23 Apr 2026

  • Jean-Noël Barrot's conversation with Mr Roberto Velasco Alvarez, Secretary of Foreign Affairs of Mexico France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 23 Apr 2026

  • Press conference - Sydney Prime Minister of Australia 23 Apr 2026

  • International Day of Multilateralism and Diplomacy for Peace: Statement by the High Representative Kaja Kallas European External Action Service (EEAS) 23 Apr 2026 -- The world stands at a critical juncture. The international order we have known for 80 years is under growing pressure. International law is being bent, ignored or openly violated. The institutions designed to uphold peace and security are being challenged — including by those who once helped to build them.

  • World News in Brief: Civilians at risk in Gaza and West Bank, Duterte to go on trial in ICC, Yemen detainees update UN News 23 Apr 2026 -- Continued strikes, shelling and gunfire in residential areas of Gaza are raising alarm over civilian safety, the UN relief coordination office OCHA has warned.
